topic: Part 8b — Dome Author's Fair-Auditor List — post-integration prose review
our_argument_summary: Part 8b adjudicates the dome author's 5-item 'What A Fair Auditor Should Actually Test' checklist (review-response.html, 2026-04-17). Adopts 3 in full (claim_key annotations partial, sequenced-read at WIN-069 as steel-man, four-layer eclipse stack), 1 in part, rejects Point 5 ('only current wording') in favor of a stricter symmetric replacement standard (dated citations + always-current engagement + preserved change log). Uses a (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) frame per point with the (c)-under-adoption clause as the substantive hero. Closing 8b.6 notes the same-window sequencing of Point 5's publication with API concessions; 8b.7 commits to a symmetric standard.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Not directly applicable — this is a prose section adjudicating an externally published list, not a code-validated WIN. The relevant cross-file checks are (a) commits 5021eec / 6e06efb / a57e731 in the dome repo, (b) chg-20260417-1620-001..006 in our own poller archive, (c) live verbatim of the five points on review-response.html.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: 8b.3 conditional 'If the dome has not pinned the threshold to this precision, we cite the gap as our finding' and 8b.4 conditional 'If the dome has not defined successful Tier 3 result quantitatively, we cite that as the operative gap' read as escape clauses rather than findings — the conditional has not been evaluated and resolved in the prose. A 'gap finding' that only triggers if-not is a contingent finding, not a delivered one.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None directly in 8b — all named commits and change files verify against the repo. But two 'Under adoption we add X' claims (claim_key fields on WIN-058/058b/062 in 8b.1; Resolution criteria sentence on WIN-058b in 8b.4) describe edits to data/wins.json that have NOT been shipped — those rows currently lack claim_key and the resolution-criteria sentence.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section 8b is editorial prose with no monitor.py code path; no fetch/observation pipeline applies.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Not a science-claim section; relabels-standard tag is N/A but recorded false to keep the schema valid.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Section 8b was authored AFTER the dome's 2026-04-17 review-response.html publication; this is correctly responsive (post-hoc by design — that's the entire point of an adjudication section).

holes_found.description: Two '4.2' subsection headings collide in part4. The original '4.2 The August 2026 Eclipse: A Sophisticated But Non-Discriminating Prediction' (with 4.2.1–4.2.6 children) appears first in document order; the newer '4.2 Registry Framing Audit — API Concessions vs Visible Headline' appears later. Part 8b cross-references 'Part 4.2 (Registry Framing Audit)' three times (8b.6 final paragraph; 8b.7 closing cross-reference list line 52; the 8b.0 preamble's structural reliance on 'the general API-vs-human-text pattern in Part 4.2'). Any reader using Ctrl-F or scanning the Part 4 TOC for '4.2' lands on the Eclipse section, not the Registry Framing Audit. The prior EXP-184-proposal review (hole #4) flagged this exact collision and recommended renumbering one of the two 4.2's; the integration of part8b reactivated the references without resolving the collision. Each new cross-reference compounds the misdirection.
holes_found.recommendation: Either renumber the Registry Framing Audit to the next available slot (4.6) and update part8b's three plain-text 'Part 4.2' references accordingly, or convert all three Part 8b references into hyperlinked anchor jumps (e.g., href='#registry-framing-audit' with onclick=showTab('pages')) so that text-based ambiguity does not matter. Pick one fix and apply consistently in part8b AND any other section that cross-references this audit.

holes_found.description: The 'adoption-label' CSS class is used five times in part8b ('ADOPTED (PARTIAL)', 'ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man)', 'ADOPTED (FULL)' x2, 'REJECTED (with replacement standard)') but no CSS rule for .adoption-label is defined anywhere in docs/index.html (single style block grep, plus full-tree grep across css/html/js). The five paragraphs render as plain prose with no visual distinction. The section's structural logic depends on each subsection ending with a clear adopt/reject label that a skim-reader can scan; without styling the labels visually disappear into the surrounding paragraphs, defeating the design intent.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a CSS rule for .adoption-label in the docs/index.html <style> block — e.g., display:inline-block; background:#f0f4f8; border-left:4px solid #28728b; padding:0.4em 0.8em; margin-top:0.6em; font-size:0.95em; — or specify a rule that visually distinguishes the adopt/reject summary line from body prose. Alternatively, rename to an existing styled class (e.g., one of the ks- or ps- summary classes) if such a class already provides the intended visual treatment.

holes_found.description: 8b.5 (d) and 8b.7 closing both claim the three-commitment replacement standard ('every citation carries an explicit "as of [date]" label; when cited content updates, we engage the update on its own terms in the same section, alongside the dated original; we preserve the change log') is 'already in force across this review.' This is an unverified self-claim. No audit confirming that all citations across data/sections.json carry 'as of [date]' labels has been performed. Spot-checks of recent integrations (Section 4.1 EXP-182 update, Section 2b.5 apr18 update) show that several citations to dome content do NOT carry explicit 'as of [date]' tags — they cite by URL alone. The prior EXP-184-proposal review hole #6 flagged exactly this; the integrated prose did not soften the claim or file a follow-up audit issue.
holes_found.recommendation: Two options. (A) Soften: replace 'This is already in force across this review; this section makes it explicit' (8b.5) and 'A review that cites with dates, engages current content, and preserves the trajectory is doing more work than a review that cites only the latest version' (8b.7) with 'We commit to this standard going forward; an audit of pre-2026-04-18 citations to bring them into compliance is tracked as ISS-XXXX.' Then file the issue. (B) Audit: produce a citation-compliance audit before 8b ships claiming the standard is in force, list any non-compliant citations as known exceptions, and link the audit from 8b.7.

holes_found.description: 8b.4 (c) reads as if WIN-058b already contains the 'Resolution criteria: (i) independent L1A reproduction... (ii) a successful Tier 3 eclipse-gravity measurement...' sentence ('Under adoption we add an explicit pass threshold to WIN-058b: Resolution criteria: ...'). data/wins.json's WIN-058b currently does NOT contain this sentence (verified by field-level grep — none of claim/finding/detail_claim/detail_evidence/detail_verdict_text/detail_extra contains 'Resolution criteria' or 'pass threshold' or this specific text). The 8b.7 schedule says it 'can ship immediately' as a low-risk additive edit, so the prose accurately describes a planned change — but the framing 'Under adoption we add X' immediately followed by the verbatim text reads as if the sentence is already added. A defender notes: 'Your section publishes content as if it has been delivered; in fact you are publishing aspirations.'
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) ship the resolution-criteria sentence into WIN-058b's detail_extra now (the 8b.7 schedule already says it should ship immediately), then 8b.4 (c) becomes accurate retroactively; or (b) reword 8b.4 (c) to make the conditional explicit: 'Under adoption (scheduled in 8b.7) WIN-058b would gain the following pass threshold:'. Same fix applies to 8b.1 (c)'s claim_key annotation language since claim_key fields are also unshipped on WIN-058/058b/062.

holes_found.description: 8b.3 L2 description: 'L2 (WIN-011): current verdict Not Demonstrated — the 10–13 µGal signal is within 1997-era instrumental noise; Van Camp et al. (2001) found no eclipse gravity signal with four superconducting gravimeters.' This conflates two different gravity figures. WIN-011 itself describes the Wang et al. (2000, Phys. Rev. D) 1997 Mohe measurement of '6–7 μGal valleys at first and last contact'. The '10–13 µGal' figure belongs to PRED-TIER-2 (the dome's predicted gravity-pulse signature for the 2026 eclipse). Stating that WIN-011 is 'Not Demonstrated because 10–13 µGal is within 1997-era noise' fuses the historical Wang signal with the dome's prospective prediction. The Van Camp critique is correctly applied (it is the strongest argument), but the µGal figure mislabels which signal it covers.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the µGal figure with the WIN-011-correct one: 'the 6–7 µGal Wang signal is within 1997-era LaCoste-Romberg instrumental noise; Van Camp et al. (2001) found no eclipse gravity signal with four superconducting gravimeters (~1,000× more precise).' If 8b.3 wants to attack the dome's predicted 10–13 µGal range too, do so explicitly under L3b (PRED-TIER-2), not under L2 (WIN-011).

holes_found.description: 8b.2 (c) Row 1 closer: 'Row 1 is the only link with primary data; Rows 2–4 are interpretive layers.' This overstates. Row 2 (V13 scaffold) does involve additional primary data — the AGD66/GDA94 datum coordinates and Australian survey-marker positions are independently measured geodetic anchor points, not just road distances. Rows 3 (southern validation) and 4 (metric expansion killshot) are presentational, but Row 2 has genuine geodetic primary data — which the dome model misuses (per WIN-069's argument), but it is still data. A defender will pull on this to argue the chain has more empirical anchoring than Part 8b admits.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten to: 'Row 1 is the only link whose primary data the dome model relies on for its 6.2% RMSE figure; Row 2 references geodetic datum data but uses it to derive a coordinate transform rather than test a topological prediction; Rows 3–4 are presentational layers built on Rows 1–2.' Preserve the substantive point (dependency chain in preferred order is not an independence check) while not understating Row 2's data content.

holes_found.description: 8b.3 L1 (WIN-068): 'the dome adds no derivation — only a post-hoc scaling formula calibrated against observed eclipse data.' WIN-068's own detail_evidence does not substantiate the 'post-hoc scaling formula calibrated against eclipse data' claim — it argues that Chapman's mechanism explains the signal without dome parameters and that WIN-068 is a relabel/aggregation (relabels_standard:true, post_hoc:true, derives_from_dome:false in WIN-068's code_analysis tags). The 'calibrated scaling formula' phrasing in 8b.3 implies a specific empirical procedure (fit-against-data) that WIN-068's evidence has not documented. A defender asks: 'Show me the calibration step you say happened.'
holes_found.recommendation: Soften to align with WIN-068's evidence: 'the dome adds no derivation from dome geometry — Chapman (1933) ionospheric mechanism predicts the 39-eclipse ensemble signal without dome parameters; WIN-068 reuses the Kim & Chang (2018) ensemble dataset as confirmation of a phenomenon the standard model already predicts.' Drop 'post-hoc scaling formula calibrated against observed data' unless WIN-068's evidence is updated to document the specific calibration claim.

holes_found.description: 8b.0 preamble: 'For each point we answer four things: (a) what he asks; (b) the charitable read; (c) what specifically changes in our text under adoption; (d) adopt / modify / reject, with reasons. The hero is (c). If adoption changes nothing, we say so.' The sentence 'The hero is (c)' is internal editor jargon (priority/emphasis terminology used during content drafting). Public readers do not expect to be told which clause is the 'hero' — the prose itself should make (c) carry the weight. The prior EXP-184-proposal review (hole #11) flagged this exact phrase; integration left it in.
holes_found.recommendation: Remove the sentence or reword to a public-facing equivalent: 'The substantive adjudication lives in (c) — if adoption changes nothing, we say so.' The (c)-clause emphasis can be carried by formatting (bolding the (c) label) rather than meta-commentary.

holes_found.description: 8b.2 (c): 'The Nullarbor Plain straight-road segment deviates only 0.4% from the globe geodesic; most of the 6.2% RMSE measures road curviness, not dome geometry.' The 0.4% figure is unsourced. WIN-069 detail_evidence says 'The Nullarbor Plain's road-to-geodesic ratio approaches 1.00 on its dead-straight central segment (~146 km), though the full Norseman–Ceduna crossing computes closer to ~1.07' — never gives a 0.4% figure. The prior EXP-184-proposal review (hole #8) flagged the same number; integration carried it forward unsourced. A defender pulling the 0.4% number against WIN-069's source text will find no derivation.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) source the 0.4% figure (cite the underlying ~146 km segment ratio computation that produces it), or (b) align with WIN-069's wording: 'The Nullarbor Plain's straight-road segment has near-zero deviation (~0% to ~7% across the full Norseman–Ceduna crossing depending on segment length); most of the 6.2% RMSE measures road curviness on longer routes, not dome geometry.' Option (b) is safer — it matches our own evidence base.

holes_found.description: 8b.6 cites 'monitor/changes/chg-20260417-1620-001.json through chg-20260417-1620-005.json, detected 2026-04-17T16:21:52Z' — but a sixth file chg-20260417-1620-006.json (classification 'strategic', same detection timestamp 2026-04-17T16:21:52Z, same poller batch) also exists in the change archive. Capping the citation at 005 understates the magnitude of the 24-hour rewrite by one file. Minor citation imprecision — the 005-cap was likely an artifact of the EXP-184 draft being written before chg-006 was archived.
holes_found.recommendation: Update the cited range to 'chg-20260417-1620-001.json through chg-20260417-1620-006.json' and add a one-clause note describing what 006 covers (it appears to enumerate killshot.html badge demotions and falsification-rule rewrite per the file summary).

holes_found.description: PRED-095 and PRED-TIER-1 in data/predictions.json are functionally the same magnetic falsifier — both '−17 to −21 nT' for the Aug 2026 eclipse, both registered 2026-04-04, both close 2026-08-12, both pending. 8b.3 L3a names PRED-095 only; PRED-TIER-1 is unaddressed. A reader cross-checking against predictions.json finds two near-identical entries for the same predicted phenomenon and no disambiguation in the section. The prior EXP-184-proposal review separated L3 into L3a/L3b correctly but did not address whether PRED-095 vs PRED-TIER-1 is one underlying claim with two IDs or two distinct claims.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) add a one-sentence footnote/parenthetical in 8b.3 L3a noting that PRED-095 and PRED-TIER-1 cover the same magnetic falsifier under different identifier conventions (the dome's Tier-N taxonomy vs. its sequential PRED numbering), or (b) file an issue against data/predictions.json to consolidate or explicitly cross-reference the two entries. The section needn't carry the full disambiguation, but it should not leave a reader wondering.

holes_found.description: 8b.3 L3a publishes the dome's magnetic falsifier ('Pass = observed -17 to -21 nT at >80% coverage stations, ≥3 independent sites, pre-registered instruments; Fail = outside this range, or signal explained by ionospheric SQ variation alone') without applying our own non-discrimination thesis to it. The -17 to -21 nT Pass range IS the Chapman (1933) ionospheric SQ-dynamo prediction, calibrated against the same Kim & Chang (2018) ensemble that WIN-068 draws from (WIN-068 finding field: 'Non-discriminating: Chapman (1933) ionospheric mechanism predicts eclipse magnetic perturbations across all 39 eclipses in the ensemble'; Part 4.2 Eclipse title: 'A Sophisticated But Non-Discriminating Prediction'). A Pass in PRED-095's stated band does not distinguish dome from globe — both predict signals in that range; only the dome claims novelty in the derivation path, and WIN-068's code_analysis tag derives_from_dome:false confirms there is no independent dome derivation. The 'or signal explained by ionospheric SQ variation alone' Fail-clause is a nod toward this but reads as an escape rather than a structural acknowledgment that the Pass criterion itself is non-discriminating. As written, L3a strengthens the dome's framing of PRED-095 as a meaningful falsifier more than it applies our own architectural critique. Same structural argument we already make about WIN-068 and Part 4.2 Eclipse, forwarded into new prose without being applied. Steel-man counter (and its rebuttal): the dome may argue that -17 to -21 nT at >80% coverage with pre-registered instruments is a sharper quantitative threshold than Chapman generically predicts; the rebuttal is that the dome's threshold is calibrated on Kim & Chang 2018 (the same ensemble), so the specificity is a product of shared training data, not independent derivation — and the derives_from_dome:false tag forecloses the 'we have our own derivation' claim.
holes_found.recommendation: Three-part edit, all in 8b.3. (1) Body — add one explicit sentence to the under-adoption L3a paragraph immediately after the threshold publication: 'A Pass in this band does not distinguish dome from globe — both predict signals in this range; only the dome claims a novel derivation path, and WIN-068 code_analysis (derives_from_dome:false) shows there is no independent dome derivation to distinguish.' (2) Closer — extend the (d) 'Unchanged:' clause from one item to two: 'Unchanged: (a) an ensemble anchor fit to the data it "anchors" is the OTS-wrong-side pattern in physics form — the right operation applied to the wrong side; (b) L3a's Pass band is the Chapman (1933) ionospheric prediction calibrated on the same Kim & Chang (2018) ensemble — a Pass does not distinguish dome from globe.' (3) Adoption label — change 'ADOPTED (FULL): four-layer eclipse stack; Tier 3 threshold explicit.' to 'ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man): four-layer eclipse stack as audit format; ensemble-anchor and L3a non-discrimination critiques unchanged.' This parallels 8b.2's existing 'ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man)' label and signals that the layered presentation is adopted while the substantive critiques survive. Optionally also promote the non-discrimination point to L3a's TLDR so a skim-reader sees it before expanding. Optionally revisit the 'Fail = ... signal explained by ionospheric SQ variation alone' clause — it pretends SQ variation is a separable alternative when Chapman IS the SQ-dynamo mechanism; either drop the clause or acknowledge it makes the test logically unable to Pass.

holes_found.description: 8b.3 and 8b.4 both contain conditional 'gap findings' that rely on a not-yet-evaluated antecedent: 8b.3 L3a says 'If the dome has not pinned the threshold to this precision, we cite the gap as our finding'; 8b.4 (c) says 'If the dome has not defined "successful Tier 3 result" quantitatively, we cite that as the operative gap.' These are escape-clause commitments — a reader cannot tell whether the gap is real (dome threshold actually unpinned) or hypothetical (dome may have already pinned it). For each conditional, either the antecedent has been checked (and the result reported) or it hasn't (and the section publishes a contingent finding rather than a delivered one).
holes_found.recommendation: For each 'if the dome has not X' clause: actually check the dome's published threshold/definition NOW (live read of review-response.html / methodology.json / killshot.html), then either delete the conditional and report the verified gap directly, or delete the conditional and acknowledge the dome has met the threshold in question. Conditional findings that don't resolve their antecedent are weaker than they appear.

stronger_arguments.description: Same-window-as-API-concessions argument (8b.6) could be strengthened by quantifying the number of distinct file edits that landed in the 24-hour window and the cumulative line-count delta. The chg-005 summary mentions '+77 lines net across 3 commits' for ai_manifest.json and '+147/-129 in one commit' for audit.html. A one-line aggregate ('11 files modified across 3 commits totaling ~N lines added / N lines removed in the 24-hour detection window') makes the 'rewrite the API while publishing the audit rule' point quantitatively rather than rhetorically.

8b.5 (c) reductio could include a numbered count: 'Under literal adoption of "only current wording" we would retire N dated audits in Part 8 (the eleven directives), M citations in Parts 2/4/6 marked "as of [earlier date]", and the entire chg-*.json archive (currently P entries).' The numerical specificity makes the reductio harder to dismiss as rhetoric — it shows the rule's enforcement cost in concrete terms.

8b.4 could acknowledge a specific dome-defender steel-man we don't currently engage: the dome may argue that 'pending until independent L1A reproduction' is a high bar that no reasonable claim is expected to meet quickly, and that demanding it is moving the goalpost. Our reply would be: independent reproduction is the orthodox standard for a claim built on raw L1 instrument residuals. Engaging this counter explicitly strengthens 8b.4 — currently we adopt his ask without acknowledging the bar's stringency.
sources: WIN-058b detail_evidence on L1A residual cross-correlation

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author's five-point list is genuinely a step toward auditability, not a defensive maneuver. Three of the five (claim_key annotations, layered eclipse stack, explicit resolution criteria for WIN-058b) are orthodox audit hygiene that improves both his and our positions. The list also surfaces a real ambiguity (which 'WIN-058' label refers to which underlying claim) that our review had been working around without naming. Even Point 2's sequenced-read request, while it cannot resolve our independence audit, IS a legitimate way to present a dependency chain — adopting it as a steel-man addendum at WIN-069 is correct. The list is good-faith.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Adoption of three-of-five does not change the verdict structure of our review on any of the underlying WINs (WIN-068 stays Std Model Explains; WIN-011 stays Not Demonstrated; WIN-058 stays Misleading; WIN-058b stays Not Demonstrated; WIN-069 stays Misleading). The list is a methodological improvement layer, not a re-evaluation of the science — every claim it touches survives the more careful audit it requests. Point 5's rejection (with replacement) preserves the dated record, which is itself evidence. The fair-auditor list is consistent with our findings; it does not move them.

text_fingerprint.notes: 8 subsections (8b.0 preamble, 8b.1-8b.5 five-point adjudication, 8b.6 sequencing meta, 8b.7 closing commitment); 5 adoption-label paragraphs (1 partial, 3 full, 1 rejected-with-replacement).

deeper_analysis: Part 8b succeeds as a structural move — it adopts a charitable, public-facing adjudication frame for an externally-published list of audit criteria. The (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) per-point pattern is good: (a) names the ask, (b) gives charitable read, (c) shows concrete text changes, (d) returns the verdict. The hero/(c) clause IS the right structural choice. The four major risks are (1) the unfixed 4.2 numbering collision compounds with each new cross-reference; (2) the section publishes commitments (claim_key fields, resolution-criteria sentence, sequenced-read table at WIN-069, four-row eclipse stack) but does not ship the underlying changes — a defender will note we adopt his asks rhetorically without the data-file edits; (3) the 'this standard is already in force' claim in 8b.5 / 8b.7 is unverified; (4) the missing adoption-label CSS makes the section's structural visual logic invisible. None of these is critical — none hands a defender our verdict on any underlying WIN. But they are the four things a careful adversarial reader will pull on first. Recommend: minor_edit covering the seven moderate-and-below issues; file separate issues for the 4.2 collision (renumbering decision) and the citation-compliance audit; ship claim_key + resolution-criteria + adoption-label CSS as low-risk additive edits. ADDED 2026-04-18 post-review (operator question): a fifth structural risk — 8b.3 L3a publishes PRED-095's magnetic Pass threshold without applying our own non-discrimination thesis (Chapman predicts the same -17 to -21 nT band; Pass does not distinguish dome from globe). This is the same critique WIN-068 finding and Part 4.2 Eclipse title carry; L3a forwards the prediction into new prose without forwarding the critique. Upgraded to major because it weakens the section's adversarial posture against an eclipse claim we already characterize as non-discriminating elsewhere.

citations_verified: WIN-069 verdict 'Misleading'; finding text matches '6.2% RMSE is 10,000x worse than geodetic standards; AGD66->GDA94 shift is routine datum improvement' (data/wins.json)
WIN-011 verdict 'Not Demonstrated'; Van Camp et al. (2001) reference matches detail_evidence (data/wins.json)
PRED-095 registered 2026-04-04, window closes 2026-08-12, verdict pending (data/predictions.json)
Section 8.12 'Directive #11: AI Auditors Should Accept the Model as Established' exists in part8 (data/sections.json)
Section 8.17 'From Misleading AI to Managing AI — The April 2026 Escalation' exists in part8 (data/sections.json)
Anchor #timestamp-error and showTab('timestamp') exist in docs/index.html

Commits 5021eec (review-response.html), 6e06efb (methodology.json + claim_index.json registry_integrity_notes), a57e731 (workflow scripts) verified against monitor/changes/chg-20260417-1620-*.json summaries
TOC entry at line 1368 of build-scripts/generate-html.js wired correctly to part8b with showTab('ai')
citations_failed: 0.4% Nullarbor figure not derivable from WIN-069 detail_evidence (the source says 'approaches 1.00 on its ~146 km central segment' — no 0.4% number) — 8b.2 (c)
10–13 µGal figure attributed to WIN-011's 1997-era noise context but WIN-011 actually concerns Wang's 6–7 µGal measurement; 10–13 µGal belongs to PRED-TIER-2 — 8b.3 L2
'Resolution criteria: (i)... (ii)...' sentence claimed as added to WIN-058b (8b.4 (c)) — not present in data/wins.json field-level grep
claim_key fields claimed as added to WIN-058 / WIN-058b / WIN-062 (8b.1 (c)) — fields absent from current data/wins.json
citations_unchecked: Live verbatim of the five points on review-response.html (claimed verified per 8b.0 'Verified verbatim against the live review-response.html on 2026-04-18') — not independently re-verified in this curmudgeon run; would require external HTTP fetch
WIN-069 'AGD66->GDA94 shift is routine datum improvement' factual basis vs primary geodetic literature — accepted from WIN-069 detail_evidence, not independently verified against geodetic textbook
Chapman (1933) Terrestrial Magnetism and Atmospheric Electricity 38:3 — citation accepted from WIN-068 detail_evidence, DOI not separately checked
Kim & Chang (2018) Advances in Space Research 61:2040–2054 — citation accepted from WIN-068 detail_evidence, DOI not separately checked

change_summary: Initial post-integration cycle review of part8b (queue_id 179, EXP-184 integrated). Compared against pre-integration EXP-184-proposal review (queue_id 178, 11 prior holes). Five prior holes verifiably fixed during integration (Kill-Shot misattribution, L3 PRED muddle, EXP-183 stale text, multi-file push-window terminology, 8b.4 TLDR verdicts). Six prior holes carry forward unfixed (two 4.2 collision, 8b.5 'in force' claim, 0.4% Nullarbor source, hero in 8b.0, claim_key/resolution unshipped). New findings added: missing adoption-label CSS, 8b.3 L2 µGal mismatch, 8b.2 Row 2 primary-data overclaim, 8b.3 L1 calibration framing, 8b.6 chg-006 omission, PRED-095 / PRED-TIER-1 disambiguation, conditional 'if the dome has not X' escape clauses. Post-review patch (operator follow-up): added 12th hole (major) — 8b.3 L3a non-discrimination of the -17 to -21 nT Pass threshold against Chapman ionospheric prediction; recommendation includes explicit sentence + TLDR promotion + Fail-clause review. Operator second-pass: extended the L3a non-discrimination hole recommendation to a three-part edit (body sentence + (d) Unchanged clause + adoption label change from 'ADOPTED (FULL)' to 'ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man)' parallel to 8b.2).

post_review_amendments.reason: operator question surfaced uncovered finding on 8b.3 L3a non-discrimination
added_holes: holes_found[11] (major) — 8b.3 L3a non-discrimination of Pass threshold against Chapman

post_review_amendments.reason: operator second-pass — adoption-label inconsistency: ADOPTED (FULL) overstates the adoption when the underlying prediction is non-discriminating
modified_holes: holes_found[11] — extended recommendation to include label change ADOPTED (FULL) -> ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man) parallel to 8b.2
